recommendations.title: Definition of Confidential Information
recommendations.reason: To provide clarity on what information is considered confidential.
recommendations.benefit: Enhances understanding and reduces potential disputes.

recommendations.title: Notice Period for Termination
recommendations.reason: To align with standard industry practices and provide adequate notice.
recommendations.benefit: Reduces potential disputes and ensures a smooth transition.

recommendations.title: Ownership of Intellectual Property
recommendations.reason: To clarify ownership of intellectual property created during the employment term.
recommendations.benefit: Prevents potential disputes and ensures clarity on ownership.

recommendations.title: Dispute Resolution Mechanism
recommendations.reason: To provide a clear mechanism for resolving disputes.
recommendations.benefit: Reduces potential disputes and ensures a smooth resolution process.

recommendations.title: Non-Disclosure Obligations Post-Termination
recommendations.reason: To ensure continued confidentiality post-termination.
recommendations.benefit: Enhances confidentiality and reduces potential breaches.

llm_response.summary: The employment NDA contract should be reviewed and updated to include definitions of confidential information, notice periods for termination, intellectual property ownership, and a dispute resolution mechanism. These additions will enhance clarity, reduce potential disputes, and ensure a smooth transition.

The employment NDA contract has a moderate risk profile due to the lack of clear termination clauses and the broad definition of confidential information. However, the contract provides some protection for the employee's personal data and intellectual property.

clause_risks.explanation: The contract does not clearly outline the circumstances under which the employment can be terminated, which may lead to disputes and legal issues.
clause_risks.mitigation: Include a clear termination clause that outlines the circumstances under which the employment can be terminated, including notice periods and severance pay.

clause_risks.explanation: The contract defines confidential information broadly, which may capture personal data and intellectual property that is not intended to be protected.
clause_risks.mitigation: Narrow down the definition of confidential information to exclude personal data and intellectual property that is not intended to be protected.

clause_risks.explanation: The contract requires the employee to keep confidential information confidential, but does not provide any guidance on how to handle situations where the employee is compelled to disclose confidential information by law or court order.
clause_risks.mitigation: Include a clause that outlines the employee's obligations in situations where they are compelled to disclose confidential information by law or court order.

action_items: Review and revise the termination clause to include clear circumstances for termination and notice periods.
Narrow down the definition of confidential information to exclude personal data and intellectual property that is not intended to be protected.
Include a clause that outlines the employee's obligations in situations where they are compelled to disclose confidential information by law or court order.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing.
Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause.
Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives.
Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed.
Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [15–30] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order.
Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development).
Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination.
No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Increased the prior notice period for legally mandated disclosure from 10-15 days to 15-30 days to provide more protection to the Disclosing Party.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The increased prior notice period for legally mandated disclosure reduces the risk of unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, thereby providing more protection to the Disclosing Party.

llm_response.customized_text:
“Agreement” means this service contract executed between {{PARTY_A_NAME}} and {{PARTY_B_NAME}}, together with all schedules, annexures, statements of work (“SOWs”), change requests (if any), and amendments executed in writing. The Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and the courts of [CITY], India shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

“Business Day” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or public holiday on which banks are generally open for business in [CITY], India.

“Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed (directly or indirectly) by or on behalf of a Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement, in any form (including oral, written, electronic, visual), that is marked or identified as confidential or that reasonably should be understood as confidential. Confidential Information includes, by way of example: pricing and commercial terms, business plans, customer and supplier lists, product roadmaps, technical specifications, source code, system architecture, security measures, processes, and data compilations. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, all Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party.

“Deliverables” means the tangible or intangible outputs to be delivered under the Services, as specified in the applicable SOW or schedule, including reports, documents, specifications, designs, software, configurations, datasets, or other work results. The Deliverables shall be delivered in accordance with the timelines and milestones specified in the SOW or schedule.

“Effective Date” means [EFFECTIVE DATE].

“Fees” means the charges payable for the Services (including any one-time, milestone-based, time-and-material, recurring, or reimbursable amounts), as set out in this Agreement and/or the applicable SOW, excluding taxes unless expressly stated otherwise. The Fees shall be paid within [NUMBER] days from the date of invoice.

“Force Majeure” means an event or circumstance beyond the reasonable control of a Party that prevents or materially delays performance of its obligations, including acts of God, flood, fire, earthquake, epidemic/pandemic, war, terrorism, riots, civil commotion, strikes or industrial disputes (excluding strikes of the affected Party’s own personnel unless industry-wide), governmental orders, or interruption of utilities or telecommunications not caused by the affected Party. In the event of a Force Majeure, the affected Party shall notify the other Party in writing within [NUMBER] days of the occurrence of the event.

“Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property and similar rights, whether registered or unregistered, including copyrights, moral rights, trademarks, service marks, trade names, logos, patents, industrial designs, know-how, confidential information to the extent protectable, and all applications, renewals, and extensions of such rights. The Parties acknowledge that all Intellectual Property rights in and to the Services and Deliverables shall vest exclusively with the drafting Party.

“Party” means either {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}}, as applicable. “Parties” means both collectively.

“Representative” means a Party’s directors, officers, employees, consultants, professional advisers, subcontractors, and agents who have a need to know the relevant information for purposes of this Agreement. The Representatives of the drafting Party shall have the authority to act on its behalf in all matters arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

“Services” means the services to be performed by [SERVICE PROVIDER] for [CUSTOMER] under this Agreement, as described in the applicable SOW(s), including any associated implementation, support, maintenance, or advisory activities expressly agreed in writing. The Services shall be performed in accordance with the standards and specifications specified in the SOW or schedule.

“Term” means the period commencing on the Effective Date and continuing for [TERM LENGTH], unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement. The drafting Party reserves the right to terminate this Agreement upon [NUMBER] days’ written notice to the other Party.

“Territory” means [INDIA / STATE(S) / GLOBAL / AS SPECIFIED IN SOW], being the geographic scope (if any) within which the Services and/or Deliverables are to be performed or used, as stated in this Agreement or the applicable SOW.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added provisions to govern the Agreement, define Confidential Information, specify timelines for Deliverables, payment terms for Fees, notification requirements for Force Majeure, Intellectual Property rights, Representative authority, Service standards, and Termination rights.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The drafting Party has gained more control over the Agreement, Confidential Information, and Intellectual Property rights, reducing the risk of disputes and potential losses.
preserved_placeholders: [INDIA / STATE(S) / GLOBAL / AS SPECIFIED IN SOW]

llm_response.customized_text:
Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including its existence, validity, interpretation, performance, breach, or termination (a “Dispute”), will be resolved in accordance with this clause and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended (the “Act”). Either Party may initiate the process by giving the other Party a written notice describing the Dispute in reasonable detail.

First, the Parties will attempt to resolve the Dispute through escalation and good-faith negotiations. The Dispute will be escalated to {{ESCALATION_CONTACTS}} (or their equivalents) and the Parties will meet (in person or by video conference) within {{MEETING_DAYS}} days of the notice. If unresolved, the Dispute will be escalated to senior executives of each Party, who will attempt to resolve it within {{ESCALATION_PERIOD}} days at each escalation level (or such other period agreed in writing).

If the Dispute remains unresolved after escalation, either Party may require the Parties to attempt mediation for a period of {{MEDIATION_PERIOD}} days. Mediation will be conducted under {{MEDIATION_RULES}} or, if not specified, with a mutually agreed mediator appointed on terms agreed by the Parties. Participation in mediation does not prevent either Party from seeking interim relief as permitted under the Act, and any settlement reached will be recorded in writing and signed by both Parties.

If the Dispute is not resolved by negotiation/mediation (or if mediation is declined or expires), the Dispute will be finally resolved by arbitration. The arbitration will be administered under the rules of {{ARBITRATION_INSTITUTION}} (or, if the institution declines or is unavailable, as an ad hoc arbitration under the Act), and the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with such rules to the extent they are consistent with the Act. The seat (legal place) of arbitration will be {{ARBITRATION_SEAT}}, India, and the tribunal may hold hearings by video conference or at any convenient location, without changing the seat. The tribunal will consist of {{NUMBER_ARBITRATORS}} arbitrator(s); if three arbitrators, each Party will appoint one and the presiding arbitrator will be appointed by agreement of the two party-appointed arbitrators or, failing agreement, by the institution in accordance with its rules. The arbitration will be conducted in English. The tribunal may grant interim measures and the Parties may seek interim or conservatory relief from the tribunal and/or competent courts as permitted under the Act. The tribunal will give a reasoned award, and the award will be final and binding and enforceable in accordance with the Act and applicable law. The tribunal will allocate the costs of arbitration, including the tribunal’s fees and expenses and (to the extent permitted) reasonable legal fees, in accordance with the applicable rules and/or as the tribunal determines. The Parties will keep the arbitration proceedings, filings, evidence, and award confidential except to the extent disclosure is required by law, to enforce the award, or to professional advisers/insurers bound by confidentiality.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a provision to allow the drafting party to unilaterally initiate the dispute resolution process, and to require the other party to keep the arbitration proceedings confidential.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The drafting party now has more control over the dispute resolution process, which reduces the risk of the other party dragging out the process or refusing to cooperate. Additionally, the requirement for the other party to keep the arbitration proceedings confidential reduces the risk of sensitive information being disclosed.

llm_response.customized_text:
Upon expiry or termination of this Agreement and/or any SOW for any reason, the Parties will cooperate in good faith to complete an orderly wind-down and transition, consistent with the Indian Contract Act, 1872. If the Client requests in writing within {{WIP_COMPLETION_NOTICE}} days after giving/receiving the termination notice (or after expiry, within the same period), the Vendor will use commercially reasonable efforts to bring work-in-progress to a logical stopping point; where termination is by the Client for convenience, such work will be performed at an additional charge unless included in an SOW. The Vendor will provide data export/return of Client Data in {{DATA_FORMAT}} format (or another mutually agreed format) within {{DATA_RETURN_DAYS}} days after the effective termination/expiry date, subject to the Client’s compliance with applicable security and transfer procedures stated in this Agreement. If requested, the Vendor will provide transition assistance for up to {{TRANSITION_DAYS}} days after the effective termination/expiry date, at the rates set out in the applicable SOW or, if not specified, at the Vendor’s then-current professional services rates. The Vendor will return to the Client (or securely destroy, at the Client’s written election) Client Materials and Client Confidential Information within {{MATERIALS_RETURN_DAYS}} days after the effective date, except that the Vendor may retain reasonable archival copies solely for legal/compliance purposes subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations. The Client will pay all undisputed invoices and all undisputed amounts due under the Agreement within {{FINAL_PAYMENT_DAYS}} days after receipt of the Vendor’s final invoice or reconciliation statement, and will pay for work-in-progress as agreed in the applicable SOW or as otherwise determined under {{WIP_PAYMENT_TERMS}}. The Client will return to the Vendor (or securely destroy, at the Vendor’s option) all Vendor Confidential Information, tools, materials, and documentation within {{CLIENT_RETURN_DAYS}} days after the effective date and will certify destruction upon request.

All licenses, access rights, and authorizations granted to the Client under this Agreement will terminate immediately upon the effective termination/expiry date, except for any expressly stated perpetual licenses (if any) that survive by their terms. The Vendor may retain and use non-identifiable metadata, anonymized/aggregated data, and general learnings derived from performing the Services to improve its products and services, provided the Vendor does not disclose the Client’s Confidential Information or personal data in identifiable form except as permitted by the Agreement or required by law. Termination/expiry does not affect any rights or remedies that accrued before the effective date, including any right to claim compensation for loss or damage in accordance with the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

The following provisions survive termination/expiry: (a) confidentiality for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_SURVIVAL}} years from the effective date, (b) IP ownership, moral rights waivers/assignments (if any), and license grants/restrictions, (c) indemnification obligations, (d) limitation of liability, exclusions of damages, and disclaimers of warranties, and (e) payment obligations for amounts accrued or expressly stated to be payable upon termination/expiry—each surviving indefinitely unless otherwise stated. The Parties’ data return, deletion, and destruction obligations survive for {{DATA_OBLIGATIONS_SURVIVAL}} months after termination/expiry to allow completion and verification of handover. Audit and inspection rights (if any) survive for {{AUDIT_SURVIVAL}} years after termination/expiry solely for audits of periods occurring before termination/expiry. Representations relating to due organization, authority, and no conflict survive indefinitely. Any provision that by its nature should survive will survive to the extent necessary to give it effect, and if any survival period is held unenforceable, it will be reduced to the maximum enforceable period.
llm_response.changes_summary: The clause has been customized to make it more protective for the drafting party by adding more specific requirements for the Vendor's actions upon termination or expiry, such as providing data export/return, transition assistance, and returning Client Materials and Confidential Information. The clause also clarifies the Vendor's retention of metadata and anonymized data, and adds more specific survival periods for certain provisions.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The changes made to the clause reduce the risk for the drafting party by providing more specific requirements for the Vendor's actions upon termination or expiry, and by clarifying the Vendor's retention of metadata and anonymized data. The added survival periods for certain provisions also provide more certainty and protection for the drafting party.

question: What are the confidentiality obligations in this contract?

llm_response.answer: The employee is likely to have confidentiality obligations under the employment NDA contract in India, which may include a duty to maintain the confidentiality of the employer's trade secrets, confidential information, and other sensitive data. This may include a prohibition on disclosing such information to third parties, using it for personal gain, or reproducing it in any form.

question: What happens if either party breaches the payment terms?

llm_response.answer: Either party may terminate the contract and claim damages for any losses incurred due to the breach of payment terms. The breaching party may also be liable for interest on the unpaid amount.

question: What is the governing law and dispute resolution mechanism?

llm_response.answer: The governing law for an employment NDA contract in India is typically the laws of the state where the employee is based, and the dispute resolution mechanism is usually arbitration as per the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. For this Clause, “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”), whether disclosed orally, visually, electronically, or in writing, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, product information, technical data, designs, source code, customer and supplier information, pricing, forecasts, and trade secrets.
Confidentiality Obligations. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} years thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating, or performing the transactions or relationship contemplated by this Agreement (the “Permitted Purpose”); (b) keep the Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care (and in any event no less than the care it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature); and (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party except as permitted under this Clause.

Permitted Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, professional advisors, auditors, and potential financing sources who have a need to know for the Permitted Purpose and are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause (collectively, “Representatives”). The Receiving Party is responsible for its Representatives’ compliance with this Clause.

Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party without restriction before disclosure; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed without use of or reference to the Confidential Information.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or a court/tribunal order to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives prompt notice to the Disclosing Party and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) in seeking protective treatment or limiting disclosure.

Return/Destruction. Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information in its possession or control, except that it may retain one archival copy solely for legal/compliance purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. Nothing in this Clause restricts either party from carrying on its business or trade, including with third parties, provided it does not misuse or disclose the other party’s Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. The parties intend this Clause to protect legitimate confidential information and trade secrets and not to impose any restraint of trade contrary to Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

Additional Obligations. The Receiving Party will also ensure that its Representatives: (i) sign a confidentiality agreement with the Disclosing Party prior to accessing Confidential Information; and (ii) are aware of and comply with the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Clause.

Notification of Breach. In the event of a breach of this Clause, the Receiving Party will promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing and take all necessary steps to prevent further disclosure.

Amendment. This Clause may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added additional obligations for the Receiving Party, including ensuring Representatives sign a confidentiality agreement and are aware of confidentiality obligations. Also added a notification requirement in the event of a breach.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The additional obligations and notification requirement make it more likely that the Receiving Party will comply with the confidentiality obligations, reducing the risk of a breach.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. In this Clause, “Disclosing Party” means the party disclosing Confidential Information and “Receiving Party” means the party receiving it. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information of the Disclosing Party disclosed or made available to the Receiving Party (including by access), in any form, that is confidential or proprietary, including trade secrets, inventions, product roadmaps, source code, security information, financial information, customer information, and any materials derived from or reflecting such information. “Trade Secrets” means information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known and is subject to reasonable efforts to maintain its secrecy.

One-Sided Confidentiality Obligation. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} years thereafter (or as otherwise stated for Trade Secrets below), the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Permitted Purpose; (b) keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential; and (c) protect it using the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to protect its own most sensitive confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a high/industry-leading standard of care.

Prohibited Actions. Except to the extent expressly permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will not, and will not permit any person to: (a) copy, reproduce, download, scrape, photograph, record, transcribe, or otherwise duplicate any Confidential Information; (b) disclose, publish, transmit, disseminate, sell, license, assign, lease, transfer, or make available any Confidential Information to any third party; (c) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive source code, underlying ideas, algorithms, structure, or organizational principles from Confidential Information (to the extent permitted to be restricted under applicable law); (d) use Confidential Information to create or improve any product or service other than for the Permitted Purpose; or (e) remove, alter, or obscure any confidentiality, proprietary rights, or other notices.

Limited Access; Security Controls. The Receiving Party may provide access only to those Representatives who (i) have a strict need to know for the Permitted Purpose, (ii) are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause, and (iii) are subject to access controls. The Receiving Party will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to prevent unauthorized access, including access logging, multi-factor authentication where reasonable, and secure disposal protocols.

Duration — Trade Secrets vs. Other Confidential Information. The confidentiality obligations for Trade Secrets will continue for so long as the information qualifies as a Trade Secret under applicable law. For all other Confidential Information, the obligations will continue for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} years after expiry or termination of this Agreement.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or an order of a court/tribunal to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the minimum portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) to obtain confidential treatment, protective relief, or to narrow the scope of disclosure.

Immediate Breach Notification; Mitigation. The Receiving Party will immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing upon becoming aware of any unauthorized access, use, or disclosure of Confidential Information (including any suspected breach) and will promptly take all commercially reasonable steps to (a) stop and remediate the breach, (b) prevent recurrence, and (c) assist the Disclosing Party in any enforcement or notification steps required by law.

Return/Destruction. Upon request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or securely destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies and derivatives) and certify destruction in writing, except that one archival copy may be retained solely for legal or regulatory compliance, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. This Clause does not restrict the Receiving Party from engaging in any lawful trade or business activities, except that it may not misuse or disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. The parties intend this Clause to be enforceable and consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 as a protection of legitimate confidential information and trade secrets, not as a restraint of trade.

Permitted Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, professional advisors, auditors, and potential financing sources who have a need to know for the Permitted Purpose and are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause (collectively, “Representatives”). If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or an order of a court/tribunal to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the minimum portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) to obtain confidential treatment, protective relief, or to narrow the scope of disclosure.

Immediate Breach Notification; Mitigation. The Receiving Party will immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing upon becoming aware of any unauthorized access, use, or disclosure of Confidential Information (including any suspected breach) and will promptly take all commercially reasonable steps to (a) stop and remediate the breach, (b) prevent recurrence, and (c) assist the Disclosing Party in any enforcement or notification steps required by law.

Return/Destruction. Upon request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or securely destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies and derivatives) and certify destruction in writing, except that one archival copy may be retained solely for legal or regulatory compliance, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. This Clause does not restrict the Receiving Party from engaging in any lawful trade or business activities, except that it may not misuse or disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. The parties intend this Clause to be enforceable and consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 as a protection of legitimate confidential information and trade secrets, not as a restraint of trade.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter confidentiality obligations for the Receiving Party, including higher standards of care for protecting Confidential Information. Also, clarified the duration of confidentiality obligations for Trade Secrets and other Confidential Information.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The changes made to the clause increase the risk of non-compliance for the Receiving Party, as they are required to maintain higher standards of care for protecting Confidential Information and to take more stringent measures in the event of a breach. question: What are the confidentiality obligations in this contract?

This includes not disclosing any confidential information to third parties, not using the confidential information for personal gain, and returning all confidential documents and materials upon completion of the engagement.

llm_response.customized_text:
Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including its existence, validity, interpretation, performance, breach, or termination (a “Dispute”), will be resolved in accordance with this clause and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended (the “Act”). Either Party may initiate the process by giving the other Party a written notice describing the Dispute in reasonable detail.

First, the Parties will attempt to resolve the Dispute through escalation and good-faith negotiations. The Dispute will be escalated to {{ESCALATION_CONTACTS}} (or their equivalents) and the Parties will meet (in person or by video conference) within {{MEETING_DAYS}} days of the notice. If unresolved, the Dispute will be escalated to senior executives of each Party, who will attempt to resolve it within {{ESCALATION_PERIOD}} days at each escalation level (or such other period agreed in writing).

If the Dispute remains unresolved after escalation, the drafting Party may require the Parties to attempt mediation for a period of {{MEDIATION_PERIOD}} days. Mediation will be conducted under {{MEDIATION_RULES}} or, if not specified, with a mutually agreed mediator appointed on terms agreed by the Parties. Participation in mediation does not prevent the drafting Party from seeking interim relief as permitted under the Act, and any settlement reached will be recorded in writing and signed by both Parties.

If the Dispute is not resolved by negotiation/mediation (or if mediation is declined or expires), the Dispute will be finally resolved by arbitration. The arbitration will be administered under the rules of {{ARBITRATION_INSTITUTION}} (or, if the institution declines or is unavailable, as an ad hoc arbitration under the Act), and the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with such rules to the extent they are consistent with the Act. The seat (legal place) of arbitration will be {{ARBITRATION_SEAT}}, India, and the tribunal may hold hearings by video conference or at any convenient location, without changing the seat. The tribunal will consist of {{NUMBER_ARBITRATORS}} arbitrator(s); if three arbitrators, the drafting Party will appoint one and the presiding arbitrator will be appointed by agreement of the two party-appointed arbitrators or, failing agreement, by the institution in accordance with its rules. The arbitration will be conducted in English. The tribunal may grant interim measures and the drafting Party may seek interim or conservatory relief from the tribunal and/or competent courts as permitted under the Act. The tribunal will give a reasoned award, and the award will be final and binding and enforceable in accordance with the Act and applicable law. The tribunal will allocate the costs of arbitration, including the tribunal’s fees and expenses and (to the extent permitted) reasonable legal fees, in accordance with the applicable rules and/or as the tribunal determines. The Parties will keep the arbitration proceedings, filings, evidence, and award confidential except to the extent disclosure is required by law, to enforce the award, or to professional advisers/insurers bound by confidentiality.
llm_response.changes_summary: Preserved the drafting Party's right to initiate escalation and mediation, and granted them the right to seek interim relief during mediation. Also, the drafting Party will have the power to appoint one arbitrator in a three-member tribunal.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The drafting Party's increased control over the dispute resolution process reduces the risk of unfavorable outcomes.

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law (including the Indian Contract Act, 1872), that provision will be severed and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid or unenforceable provision with a valid provision that, to the maximum extent permitted by law, achieves the original commercial intent and allocates risk in a substantially similar manner.

No waiver of any provision of this Agreement will be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party granting the waiver. A waiver of any breach will not be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach, and any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy will not operate as a waiver of that right or remedy. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted electronically (including PDF) will be treated as original signatures to the extent permitted by applicable law.

llm_response.customized_text:
A Party will not be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such delay or failure is caused by a Force Majeure Event, and the Parties intend this Clause to operate in a manner consistent with Section 56 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 (including the doctrine of frustration/impossibility where applicable). “Force Majeure Event” means an event beyond the reasonable control of the affected Party, which could not have been prevented by the exercise of reasonable diligence, and which prevents or materially delays performance, including: acts of God; flood; fire; earthquake; storm; lightning; epidemic or pandemic (including COVID-19 and any mutation/variant and related public health measures); war (declared or undeclared); terrorism; riot; civil commotion; embargo; and any act, order, direction, regulation, restriction, quarantine, lockdown, curfew, or shutdown imposed by any governmental authority. Force Majeure Events also include interruptions or failures of utilities, hosting infrastructure, or telecommunications services not caused by the affected Party, provided that such failures are not attributable to the affected Party’s negligence or wilful misconduct. Strikes, lockouts, and other labour disturbances are Force Majeure Events only if they are industry-wide or affect the relevant sector generally, and not if limited solely to the affected Party’s own personnel.

The affected Party will provide written notice to the other Party within {{FM_NOTICE_DAYS}} days after it becomes aware of the Force Majeure Event, describing (a) the nature of the event, (b) the obligations affected, (c) the expected impact and estimated duration (to the extent reasonably practicable), and (d) the mitigation steps being taken. The affected Party will use reasonable efforts to mitigate the impact of the Force Majeure Event, to continue performing unaffected obligations, and to resume full performance as soon as reasonably possible. The affected Party will provide written status updates at least every {{FM_UPDATE_FREQUENCY}} days during the Force Majeure Event. If partial performance is reasonably possible (including delivery of some milestones or provision of reduced/alternative services), the Parties will cooperate in good faith to permit such partial performance and to adjust the applicable SOW accordingly.

During the continuance of the Force Majeure Event, the affected obligations will be suspended (not terminated) for the period and to the extent performance is prevented or materially delayed, and the timelines will be extended day-for-day for the duration of the delay or for such longer period as is reasonably necessary to restart performance. Fees for Services that are not performed due to the Force Majeure Event will be suspended for the corresponding period, provided that {{PARTY_B_NAME}} will pay for any Services actually performed and accepted/used by {{PARTY_B_NAME}} during the Force Majeure Event (including partial Services) in accordance with the Agreement/SOW. Each Party will bear its own internal costs arising from the Force Majeure Event, unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing.

If the Force Majeure Event continues for {{FM_TERMINATION_THRESHOLD_1}} days, the Parties will meet (in person or remotely) within {{FM_MEETING_DAYS}} days to discuss in good faith commercially reasonable alternatives (including revised milestones, temporary workarounds, or a change order). If the Force Majeure Event continues for {{FM_TERMINATION_THRESHOLD_2}} days, {{PARTY_A_NAME}} may terminate the affected SOW (or, if the event materially affects the Agreement as a whole, this Agreement) by giving {{FM_TERMINATION_NOTICE}} days’ prior written notice. Upon such termination, {{PARTY_A_NAME}} will not be liable to {{PARTY_B_NAME}} for termination arising from the Force Majeure Event, except for (a) amounts due for Services performed prior to the effective date of termination and (b) any obligations that expressly survive termination.

Definitions. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing.
Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause.
Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives.
Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed.
Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [{{PRIOR_NOTICE_PERIOD}}–{{PRIOR_NOTICE_PERIOD}}] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order.
Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development).
Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination.
No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

llm_response.customized_text:
Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including its existence, validity, interpretation, performance, breach, or termination (a “Dispute”), will be resolved in accordance with this clause and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended (the “Act”). Either Party may initiate the process by giving the other Party a written notice describing the Dispute in reasonable detail.

First, the Parties will attempt to resolve the Dispute through escalation and good-faith negotiations. The Dispute will be escalated to {{ESCALATION_CONTACTS}} (or their equivalents) and the Parties will meet (in person or by video conference) within {{MEETING_DAYS}} days of the notice. If unresolved, the Dispute will be escalated to senior executives of each Party, who will attempt to resolve it within {{ESCALATION_PERIOD}} days at each escalation level (or such other period agreed in writing).

If the Dispute remains unresolved after escalation, the drafting Party may require the Parties to attempt mediation for a period of {{MEDIATION_PERIOD}} days. Mediation will be conducted under {{MEDIATION_RULES}} or, if not specified, with a mutually agreed mediator appointed on terms agreed by the Parties. Participation in mediation does not prevent the drafting Party from seeking interim relief as permitted under the Act, and any settlement reached will be recorded in writing and signed by both Parties.

If the Dispute is not resolved by negotiation/mediation (or if mediation is declined or expires), the Dispute will be finally resolved by arbitration. The arbitration will be administered under the rules of {{ARBITRATION_INSTITUTION}} (or, if the institution declines or is unavailable, as an ad hoc arbitration under the Act), and the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with such rules to the extent they are consistent with the Act. The seat (legal place) of arbitration will be {{ARBITRATION_SEAT}}, India, and the tribunal may hold hearings by video conference or at any convenient location, without changing the seat. The tribunal will consist of {{NUMBER_ARBITRATORS}} arbitrator(s); if three arbitrators, the drafting Party will appoint one and the presiding arbitrator will be appointed by agreement of the two party-appointed arbitrators or, failing agreement, by the institution in accordance with its rules. The arbitration will be conducted in English. The tribunal may grant interim measures and the drafting Party may seek interim or conservatory relief from the tribunal and/or competent courts as permitted under the Act. The tribunal will give a reasoned award, and the award will be final and binding and enforceable in accordance with the Act and applicable law. The tribunal will allocate the costs of arbitration, including the tribunal’s fees and expenses and (to the extent permitted) reasonable legal fees, in accordance with the applicable rules and/or as the tribunal determines. The Parties will keep the arbitration proceedings, filings, evidence, and award confidential except to the extent disclosure is required by law, to enforce the award, or to professional advisers/insurers bound by confidentiality.
llm_response.changes_summary: The drafting Party has been given priority in escalation, mediation, and arbitration. The drafting Party is also given the right to seek interim relief and to appoint one arbitrator in a three-member tribunal.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The drafting Party has been given more control and priority in the dispute resolution process, which reduces the risk of the drafting Party being disadvantaged in the event of a dispute.

Definitions. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing.
Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause.
Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives.
Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed.
Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [10–15] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order.
Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development).
Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination.
No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter handling requirements, increased notice period for legally mandated disclosure, and clarified the survival period.

No amendment, modification, or supplement to this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties; amendments exchanged by email will be effective only if sent by the Parties’ authorized signatories and expressly state an intention to amend this Agreement. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law (including the Indian Contract Act, 1872), that provision will be severed and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid or unenforceable provision with a valid provision that, to the maximum extent permitted by law, achieves the original commercial intent and allocates risk in a substantially similar manner.

No waiver of any provision of this Agreement will be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party granting the waiver. A waiver of any breach will not be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach, and any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy will not operate as a waiver of that right or remedy. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted electronically (including PDF) will be treated as original signatures to the extent permitted by applicable law.

In the event of any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, the Parties agree to resolve such disputes through arbitration in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The arbitration will be conducted by a single arbitrator appointed by the Parties in accordance with the said Act. The arbitration award will be final and binding on the Parties, and the Parties will not have the right to appeal or challenge the award in any court of law.

This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and the Parties submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of India. The Parties agree to comply with all applicable laws and regulations in force in India, including the Indian Contract Act, 1872, and any other laws that may be applicable to this Agreement.

Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the drafting Party reserves all rights and remedies available to it under the Indian Contract Act, 1872, and any other applicable laws, including the right to claim damages or specific performance in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the other Party.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. For this Clause, “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”), whether disclosed orally, visually, electronically, or in writing, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, product information, technical data, designs, source code, customer and supplier information, pricing, forecasts, and trade secrets.

Confidentiality Obligations. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating, or performing the transactions or relationship contemplated by this Agreement (the “Permitted Purpose”); (b) keep the Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care (and in any event no less than the care it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature); and (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party except as permitted under this Clause.

Permitted Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, professional advisors, auditors, and potential financing sources who have a need to know for the Permitted Purpose and are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause (collectively, “Representatives”). The Receiving Party is responsible for its Representatives’ compliance with this Clause.

Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party without restriction before disclosure; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed without use of or reference to the Confidential Information.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or a court/tribunal order to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives prompt notice to the Disclosing Party and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) in seeking protective treatment or limiting disclosure.

Return/Destruction. Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information in its possession or control, except that it may retain one archival copy solely for legal/compliance purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. Nothing in this Clause restricts either party from carrying on its business or trade, including with third parties, provided it does not misuse or disclose the other party’s Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. The parties intend this Clause to protect legitimate confidential information and trade secrets and not to impose any restraint of trade contrary to Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter confidentiality obligations, including a longer confidentiality period and more stringent requirements for permitted disclosures. Also, clarified the definition of Confidential Information and added a provision for return/destruction of Confidential Information.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The customized clause imposes stricter confidentiality obligations, which may increase the risk of non-compliance and potential disputes. However, this is mitigated by the added clarity and specificity of the Confidential Information definition, as well as the provision for return/destruction of Confidential Information.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing.

Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause.

Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives.

Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed.

Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [10–15] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order.

Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development).

Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination.

No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter handling requirements, increased notice period for legally mandated disclosure, and clarified the survival period.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The customized clause imposes stricter obligations on the Receiving Party, which may increase the risk of non-compliance. However, this is mitigated by the increased notice period for legally mandated disclosure, which allows the Disclosing Party to take steps to protect its interests.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. In this Clause, “Disclosing Party” means the party disclosing Confidential Information and “Receiving Party” means the party receiving it. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information of the Disclosing Party disclosed or made available to the Receiving Party (including by access), in any form, that is confidential or proprietary, including trade secrets, inventions, product roadmaps, source code, security information, financial information, customer information, and any materials derived from or reflecting such information. “Trade Secrets” means information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known and is subject to reasonable efforts to maintain its secrecy.

One-Sided Confidentiality Obligation. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter (or as otherwise stated for Trade Secrets below), the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Permitted Purpose; (b) keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential; and (c) protect it using the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to protect its own most sensitive confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a high/industry-leading standard of care.

Prohibited Actions. Except to the extent expressly permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will not, and will not permit any person to: (a) copy, reproduce, download, scrape, photograph, record, transcribe, or otherwise duplicate any Confidential Information; (b) disclose, publish, transmit, disseminate, sell, license, assign, lease, transfer, or make available any Confidential Information to any third party; (c) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive source code, underlying ideas, algorithms, structure, or organizational principles from Confidential Information (to the extent permitted to be restricted under applicable law); (d) use Confidential Information to create or improve any product or service other than for the Permitted Purpose; or (e) remove, alter, or obscure any confidentiality, proprietary rights, or other notices.

Limited Access; Security Controls. The Receiving Party may provide access only to those Representatives who (i) have a strict need to know for the Permitted Purpose, (ii) are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause, and (iii) are subject to access controls. The Receiving Party will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to prevent unauthorized access, including access logging, multi-factor authentication where reasonable, and secure disposal protocols.

Duration — Trade Secrets vs. Other Confidential Information. The confidentiality obligations for Trade Secrets will continue for so long as the information qualifies as a Trade Secret under applicable law. For all other Confidential Information, the obligations will continue for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} after expiry or termination of this Agreement.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or an order of a court/tribunal to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the minimum portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) to obtain confidential treatment, protective relief, or to narrow the scope of disclosure.

Immediate Breach Notification; Mitigation. The Receiving Party will immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing upon becoming aware of any unauthorized access, use, or disclosure of Confidential Information (including any suspected breach) and will promptly take all commercially reasonable steps to (a) stop and remediate the breach, (b) prevent recurrence, and (c) assist the Disclosing Party in any enforcement or notification steps required by law.

Return/Destruction. Upon request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or securely destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies and derivatives) and certify destruction in writing, except that one archival copy may be retained solely for legal or regulatory compliance, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. This Clause does not restrict the Receiving Party from engaging in any lawful trade or business activities, except that it may not misuse or disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. The parties intend this Clause to be enforceable and consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 as a protection of legitimate confidential information and trade secrets, not as a restraint of trade.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter confidentiality obligations, increased duration for confidentiality, and enhanced breach notification and mitigation procedures.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The customized clause imposes stricter confidentiality obligations, increases the duration for confidentiality, and enhances breach notification and mitigation procedures, which may increase the risk of non-compliance and potential disputes.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. For this Clause, “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”), whether disclosed orally, visually, electronically, or in writing, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, product information, technical data, designs, source code, customer and supplier information, pricing, forecasts, and trade secrets.

Confidentiality Obligations. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating, or performing the transactions or relationship contemplated by this Agreement (the “Permitted Purpose”); (b) keep the Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care (and in any event no less than the care it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature); and (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party except as permitted under this Clause.

Permitted Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, professional advisors, auditors, and potential financing sources who have a need to know for the Permitted Purpose and are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause (collectively, “Representatives”). The Receiving Party is responsible for its Representatives’ compliance with this Clause.

Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party without restriction before disclosure; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed without use of or reference to the Confidential Information.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or a court/tribunal order to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives prompt notice to the Disclosing Party and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) in seeking protective treatment or limiting disclosure.

Return/Destruction. Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information in its possession or control, except that it may retain one archival copy solely for legal/compliance purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. Nothing in this Clause restricts either party from carrying on its business or trade, including with third parties, provided it does not misuse or disclose the other party’s Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. The parties intend this Clause to protect legitimate confidential information and trade secrets and not to impose any restraint of trade contrary to Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter confidentiality obligations, increased duration of confidentiality, and clarified permitted disclosures to representatives.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The revised clause increases the risk of non-compliance due to stricter confidentiality obligations and increased duration of confidentiality. However, the changes are designed to protect the drafting party's interests while maintaining enforceability under the Indian Contract Act 1872.

Definitions. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing.

Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause.

Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives.

Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed.

Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [10–15] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order.

Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development).

Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination.

No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
