If the Client requests in writing within [{{WIP_COMPLETION_NOTICE}}] days after giving/receiving the termination notice (or after expiry, within the same period), the Vendor will use commercially reasonable efforts to bring work-in-progress to a logical stopping point; where termination is by the Client for convenience, such work will be performed at an additional charge unless included in an SOW. The Vendor will provide data export/return of Client Data in [{{DATA_FORMAT}}] format (or another mutually agreed format) within [{{DATA_RETURN_DAYS}}] days after the effective termination/expiry date, subject to the Client’s compliance with applicable security and transfer procedures stated in this Agreement. If requested, the Vendor will provide transition assistance for up to [{{TRANSITION_DAYS}}] days after the effective termination/expiry date, at the rates set out in the applicable SOW or, if not specified, at the Vendor’s then-current professional services rates. The Vendor will return to the Client (or securely destroy, at the Client’s written election) Client Materials and Client Confidential Information within [{{MATERIALS_RETURN_DAYS}}] days after the effective date, except that the Vendor may retain reasonable archival copies solely for legal/compliance purposes subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations. The Client will pay all undisputed invoices and all undisputed amounts due under the Agreement within [{{FINAL_PAYMENT_DAYS}}] days after receipt of the Vendor’s final invoice or reconciliation statement, and will pay for work-in-progress as agreed in the applicable SOW or as otherwise determined under [WIP PAYMENT TERMS]. The Client will return to the Vendor (or securely destroy, at the Vendor’s option) all Vendor Confidential Information, tools, materials, and documentation within [{{CLIENT_RETURN_DAYS}}] days after the effective date and will certify destruction upon request.

All licenses, access rights, and authorizations granted to the Client under this Agreement will terminate immediately upon the effective termination/expiry date, except for any expressly stated perpetual licenses (if any) that survive by their terms. The Vendor may retain and use non-identifiable metadata, anonymized/aggregated data, and general learnings derived from performing the Services to improve its products and services, provided the Vendor does not disclose the Client’s Confidential Information or personal data in identifiable form except as permitted by the Agreement or required by law. Termination/expiry does not affect any rights or remedies that accrued before the effective date, including any right to claim compensation for loss or damage in accordance with the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

The following provisions survive termination/expiry: (a) confidentiality for [{{CONFIDENTIALITY_SURVIVAL}}] years from the effective date, (b) IP ownership, moral rights waivers/assignments (if any), and license grants/restrictions, (c) indemnification obligations, (d) limitation of liability, exclusions of damages, and disclaimers of warranties, and (e) payment obligations for amounts accrued or expressly stated to be payable upon termination/expiry—each surviving indefinitely unless otherwise stated. The Parties’ data return, deletion, and destruction obligations survive for [{{DATA_OBLIGATIONS_SURVIVAL}}] months after termination/expiry to allow completion and verification of handover. Audit and inspection rights (if any) survive for [{{AUDIT_SURVIVAL}}] years after termination/expiry solely for audits of periods occurring before termination/expiry. Representations relating to due organization, authority, and no conflict survive indefinitely. Any provision that by its nature should survive will survive to the extent necessary to give it effect, and if any survival period is held unenforceable, it will be reduced to the maximum enforceable period.

llm_response.customized_text: This Agreement, together with all SOWs, schedules, exhibits, and appendices expressly incorporated by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous proposals, bids, discussions, negotiations, understandings, and agreements, whether oral or written. Each Party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it has not relied on any representation, warranty, statement, or promise not expressly set out in this Agreement. In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and an SOW, the SOW will prevail only for the specific Services/Deliverables and commercial terms expressly covered by that SOW, and this Agreement will prevail for all general terms. The drafting Party reserves the right to modify or update this Agreement at any time, and such modifications will be effective upon notice to the other Party. No amendment, modification, or supplement to this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties; amendments exchanged by email will be effective only if sent by the Parties’ authorized signatories and expressly state an intention to amend this Agreement. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law (including the Indian Contract Act, 1872), that provision will be severed and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid or unenforceable provision with a valid provision that, to the maximum extent permitted by law, achieves the original commercial intent and allocates risk in a substantially similar manner. The drafting Party will not be liable for any damages or losses arising from any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted electronically (including PDF) will be treated as original signatures to the extent permitted by applicable law.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a provision to allow the drafting Party to modify or update the Agreement at any time, and also added a provision to limit the drafting Party's liability for any damages or losses arising from any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The added provisions increase the risk for the other Party, as they may be subject to changes or updates to the Agreement without notice, and also limit the drafting Party's liability for any damages or losses arising from any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy.

This Agreement, together with all SOWs, schedules, exhibits, and appendices expressly incorporated by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous proposals, bids, discussions, negotiations, understandings, and agreements, whether oral or written. Each Party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it has not relied on any representation, warranty, statement, or promise not expressly set out in this Agreement. In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and an SOW, the SOW will prevail only for the specific Services/Deliverables and commercial terms expressly covered by that SOW, and this Agreement will prevail for all general terms.

No amendment, modification, or supplement to this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties; amendments exchanged by email will be effective only if sent by the Parties’ authorized signatories and expressly state an intention to amend this Agreement. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law (including the Indian Contract Act, 1872), that provision will be severed and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid or unenforceable provision with a valid provision that, to the maximum extent permitted by law, achieves the original commercial intent and allocates risk in a substantially similar manner.

No waiver of any provision of this Agreement will be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party granting the waiver. A waiver of any breach will not be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach, and any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy will not operate as a waiver of that right or remedy. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted electronically (including PDF) will be treated as original signatures to the extent permitted by applicable law.

llm_response.customized_text: Definitions. For this Clause, “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”), whether disclosed orally, visually, electronically, or in writing, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, product information, technical data, designs, source code, customer and supplier information, pricing, forecasts, and trade secrets. Confidentiality Obligations. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating, or performing the transactions or relationship contemplated by this Agreement (the “Permitted Purpose”); (b) keep the Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care (and in any event no less than the care it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature); and (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party except as permitted under this Clause. Permitted Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, professional advisors, auditors, and potential financing sources who have a need to know for the Permitted Purpose and are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause (collectively, “Representatives”). The Receiving Party is responsible for its Representatives’ compliance with this Clause. Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party without restriction before disclosure; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed without use of or reference to the Confidential Information. Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or a court/tribunal order to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives prompt notice to the Disclosing Party and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) in seeking protective treatment or limiting disclosure. Return/Destruction. Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information in its possession or control, except that it may retain one archival copy solely for legal/compliance purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations. No Restraint of Trade. Nothing in this Clause restricts either party from carrying on its business or trade, including with third parties, provided it does not misuse or disclose the other party’s Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. The parties intend this Clause to protect legitimate confidential information and trade secrets and not to impose any restraint of trade contrary to Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. For added protection, the Receiving Party shall also ensure that all Confidential Information is stored in a secure manner, using encryption and access controls, and that all personnel with access to Confidential Information are bound by confidentiality agreements at least as protective as this Clause.

The added requirement for secure storage and personnel confidentiality agreements is consistent with Indian contract law principles of protecting confidential information.

llm_response.customized_text: Definitions. ‘Confidential Information’ means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a ‘Disclosing Party’) to the other (each, a ‘Receiving Party’) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing. Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause. The Receiving Party shall also be liable for any breach by its Representatives. Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives. Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed. Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [10–15] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order. Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development). Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination. No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. In this Clause, “Disclosing Party” means the party disclosing Confidential Information and “Receiving Party” means the party receiving it. “Confidential Information” means all non-public information of the Disclosing Party disclosed or made available to the Receiving Party (including by access), in any form, that is confidential or proprietary, including trade secrets, inventions, product roadmaps, source code, security information, financial information, customer information, and any materials derived from or reflecting such information. “Trade Secrets” means information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known and is subject to reasonable efforts to maintain its secrecy.

One-Sided Confidentiality Obligation. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter (or as otherwise stated for Trade Secrets below), the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Permitted Purpose; (b) keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential; and (c) protect it using the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to protect its own most sensitive confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a high/industry-leading standard of care.

Prohibited Actions. Except to the extent expressly permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will not, and will not permit any person to: (a) copy, reproduce, download, scrape, photograph, record, transcribe, or otherwise duplicate any Confidential Information; (b) disclose, publish, transmit, disseminate, sell, license, assign, lease, transfer, or make available any Confidential Information to any third party; (c) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive source code, underlying ideas, algorithms, structure, or organizational principles from Confidential Information (to the extent permitted to be restricted under applicable law); (d) use Confidential Information to create or improve any product or service other than for the Permitted Purpose; or (e) remove, alter, or obscure any confidentiality, proprietary rights, or other notices.

Limited Access; Security Controls. The Receiving Party may provide access only to those Representatives who (i) have a strict need to know for the Permitted Purpose, (ii) are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause, and (iii) are subject to access controls. The Receiving Party will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to prevent unauthorized access, including access logging, multi-factor authentication where reasonable, and secure disposal protocols.

Duration — Trade Secrets vs. Other Confidential Information. The confidentiality obligations for Trade Secrets will continue for so long as the information qualifies as a Trade Secret under applicable law. For all other Confidential Information, the obligations will continue for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} after expiry or termination of this Agreement.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or an order of a court/tribunal to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the minimum portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) to obtain confidential treatment, protective relief, or to narrow the scope of disclosure.

Immediate Breach Notification; Mitigation. The Receiving Party will immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing upon becoming aware of any unauthorized access, use, or disclosure of Confidential Information (including any suspected breach) and will promptly take all commercially reasonable steps to (a) stop and remediate the breach, (b) prevent recurrence, and (c) assist the Disclosing Party in any enforcement or notification steps required by law.

Return/Destruction. Upon request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or securely destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies and derivatives) and certify destruction in writing, except that one archival copy may be retained solely for legal or regulatory compliance, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. This Clause does not restrict the Receiving Party from engaging in any lawful trade or business activities, except that it may not misuse or disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. The parties intend this Clause to be enforceable and consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 as a protection of legitimate confidential information and trade secrets, not as a restraint of trade.

llm_response.customized_text: Definitions. For this Clause, “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a “Disclosing Party”) to the other (each, a “Receiving Party”), whether disclosed orally, visually, electronically, or in writing, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure, including business plans, product information, technical data, designs, source code, customer and supplier information, pricing, forecasts, and trade secrets. Confidentiality Obligations. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating, or performing the transactions or relationship contemplated by this Agreement (the “Permitted Purpose”); (b) keep the Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care (and in any event no less than the care it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature); and (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party except as permitted under this Clause. Permitted Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, professional advisors, auditors, and potential financing sources who have a need to know for the Permitted Purpose and are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause (collectively, “Representatives”). The Receiving Party is responsible for its Representatives’ compliance with this Clause. Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party without restriction before disclosure; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed without use of or reference to the Confidential Information. Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or a court/tribunal order to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives prompt notice to the Disclosing Party and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) in seeking protective treatment or limiting disclosure. Return/Destruction. Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information in its possession or control, except that it may retain one archival copy solely for legal/compliance purposes, subject to continued confidentiality obligations. No Restraint of Trade. Nothing in this Clause restricts either party from carrying on its business or trade, including with third parties, provided it does not misuse or disclose the other party’s Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement. The parties intend this Clause to protect legitimate confidential information and trade secrets and not to impose any restraint of trade contrary to Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. For added protection, the Receiving Party shall also be responsible for ensuring that its Representatives comply with the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Clause, and the Disclosing Party may audit the Receiving Party’s compliance with this Clause at any time during the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a provision to hold the Receiving Party responsible for its Representatives' compliance with the confidentiality obligations and allowed the Disclosing Party to audit the Receiving Party's compliance.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The added provision increases the risk of non-compliance and potential disputes between the parties, but it also provides additional protection for the Disclosing Party's confidential information.
llm_response.legal_notes: The added provision is consistent with Indian contract law and does not impose any restraint of trade contrary to Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. However, it is essential to ensure that the audit provision does not become overly burdensome or intrusive, as this could potentially lead to disputes between the parties.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. ‘Confidential Information’ means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a ‘Disclosing Party’) to the other (each, a ‘Receiving Party’) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing.

Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause.

Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives.

Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed.

Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [15] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order.

Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development).

Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination.

No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Increased the prior notice period for legally mandated disclosure from 10-15 days to 15 days to provide more protection to the drafting party.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The increased prior notice period may lead to a higher risk of delay in disclosure, which could be detrimental in situations where timely disclosure is required by law.
llm_response.legal_notes: This customization is consistent with Indian contract law and maintains the enforceability of the clause under the Indian Contract Act, 1872. However, it is essential to note that the increased prior notice period may lead to a higher risk of delay in disclosure, which could be detrimental in situations where timely disclosure is required by law.

llm_response.customized_text:
Definitions. In this Clause, ‘Disclosing Party’ means the party disclosing Confidential Information and ‘Receiving Party’ means the party receiving it. ‘Confidential Information’ means all non-public information of the Disclosing Party disclosed or made available to the Receiving Party (including by access), in any form, that is confidential or proprietary, including trade secrets, inventions, product roadmaps, source code, security information, financial information, customer information, and any materials derived from or reflecting such information. ‘Trade Secrets’ means information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known and is subject to reasonable efforts to maintain its secrecy.

One-Sided Confidentiality Obligation. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter (or as otherwise stated for Trade Secrets below), the Receiving Party will: (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Permitted Purpose; (b) keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential; and (c) protect it using the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to protect its own most sensitive confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a high/industry-leading standard of care.

Prohibited Actions. Except to the extent expressly permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will not, and will not permit any person to: (a) copy, reproduce, download, scrape, photograph, record, transcribe, or otherwise duplicate any Confidential Information; (b) disclose, publish, transmit, disseminate, sell, license, assign, lease, transfer, or make available any Confidential Information to any third party; (c) reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive source code, underlying ideas, algorithms, structure, or organizational principles from Confidential Information (to the extent permitted to be restricted under applicable law); (d) use Confidential Information to create or improve any product or service other than for the Permitted Purpose; or (e) remove, alter, or obscure any confidentiality, proprietary rights, or other notices.

Limited Access; Security Controls. The Receiving Party may provide access only to those Representatives who (i) have a strict need to know for the Permitted Purpose, (ii) are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Clause, and (iii) are subject to access controls. The Receiving Party will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to prevent unauthorized access, including access logging, multi-factor authentication where reasonable, and secure disposal protocols.

Duration — Trade Secrets vs. Other Confidential Information. The confidentiality obligations for Trade Secrets will continue for so long as the information qualifies as a Trade Secret under applicable law. For all other Confidential Information, the obligations will continue for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} after expiry or termination of this Agreement.

Legally Required Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or an order of a court/tribunal to disclose any Confidential Information, it may disclose only the minimum portion required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) to obtain confidential treatment, protective relief, or to narrow the scope of disclosure.

Immediate Breach Notification; Mitigation. The Receiving Party will immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing upon becoming aware of any unauthorized access, use, or disclosure of Confidential Information (including any suspected breach) and will promptly take all commercially reasonable steps to (a) stop and remediate the breach, (b) prevent recurrence, and (c) assist the Disclosing Party in any enforcement or notification steps required by law.

Return/Destruction. Upon request, the Receiving Party will promptly return or securely destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies and derivatives) and certify destruction in writing, except that one archival copy may be retained solely for legal or regulatory compliance, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

No Restraint of Trade. This Clause does not restrict the Receiving Party from engaging in any lawful trade or business activities, except that it may not misuse or disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. The parties intend this Clause to be enforceable and consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 as a protection of legitimate confidential information and trade secrets, not as a restraint of trade.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added stricter confidentiality obligations and enhanced security controls to protect the drafting party’s confidential information.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The added obligations and security controls increase the risk of non-compliance, but they also provide stronger protection for the drafting party’s confidential information.
llm_response.legal_notes: This customization maintains enforceability under the Indian Contract Act, 1872, by ensuring that the confidentiality obligations are consistent with Section 27 of the Act. However, it is essential to note that the added obligations and security controls may increase the risk of non-compliance, and the parties should carefully consider these risks before agreeing to this clause.

llm_response.customized_text: Definitions. ‘Confidential Information’ means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a ‘Disclosing Party’) to the other (each, a ‘Receiving Party’) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing. Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause; and (d) upon request of the Disclosing Party, return or destroy all Confidential Information in its possession. Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives. Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed; and (e) upon request of the Disclosing Party, provide written certification of compliance with this Clause. Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. llm_response.customized_text:
Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including its existence, validity, interpretation, performance, breach, or termination (a “Dispute”), will be resolved in accordance with this clause and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended (the “Act”). Either Party may initiate the process by giving the other Party a written notice describing the Dispute in reasonable detail.

First, the Parties will attempt to resolve the Dispute through escalation and good-faith negotiations. The Dispute will be escalated to [{{ESCALATION_CONTACTS}}] (or their equivalents) and the Parties will meet (in person or by video conference) within [MEETING_DAYS] days of the notice. If unresolved, the Dispute will be escalated to senior executives of each Party, who will attempt to resolve it within [{{ESCALATION_PERIOD}}] days at each escalation level (or such other period agreed in writing).

If the Dispute remains unresolved after escalation, the drafting Party may require the Parties to attempt mediation for a period of [{{MEDIATION_PERIOD}}] days. Mediation will be conducted under [{{MEDIATION_RULES}}] or, if not specified, with a mutually agreed mediator appointed on terms agreed by the Parties. Participation in mediation does not prevent either Party from seeking interim relief as permitted under the Act, and any settlement reached will be recorded in writing and signed by both Parties.

If the Dispute is not resolved by negotiation/mediation (or if mediation is declined or expires), the Dispute will be finally resolved by arbitration. The arbitration will be administered under the rules of [{{ARBITRATION_INSTITUTION}}] (or, if the institution declines or is unavailable, as an ad hoc arbitration under the Act), and the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with such rules to the extent they are consistent with the Act. The seat (legal place) of arbitration will be [{{ARBITRATION_SEAT}}], India, and the tribunal may hold hearings by video conference or at any convenient location, without changing the seat. The tribunal will consist of [{{NUMBER_ARBITRATORS}}] arbitrator(s); if three arbitrators, each Party will appoint one and the presiding arbitrator will be appointed by agreement of the two party-appointed arbitrators or, failing agreement, by the institution in accordance with its rules. The arbitration will be conducted in English. The tribunal may grant interim measures and the Parties may seek interim or conservatory relief from the tribunal and/or competent courts as permitted under the Act. The tribunal will give a reasoned award, and the award will be final and binding and enforceable in accordance with the Act and applicable law. The tribunal will allocate the costs of arbitration, including the tribunal’s fees and expenses and (to the extent permitted) reasonable legal fees, in accordance with the applicable rules and/or as the tribunal determines. The drafting Party will be entitled to recover its reasonable costs and expenses incurred in the arbitration process, including attorney’s fees, if the arbitration results in an award in its favor. The Parties will keep the arbitration proceedings, filings, evidence, and award confidential except to the extent disclosure is required by law, to enforce the award, or to professional advisers/insurers bound by confidentiality.

llm_response.risk_explanation: The risk has increased because the drafting Party now has more control over the dispute resolution process and can recover its costs in case of an award in its favor, which may lead to more disputes being taken to arbitration.
llm_response.legal_notes: This customization is consistent with Indian contract law, specifically the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended. The drafting Party’s entitlement to recover its costs and expenses in case of an award in its favor is a common provision in arbitration clauses to ensure that the Party bears the costs of the arbitration process.

llm_response.customized_text:
A Party will not be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent such delay or failure is caused by a Force Majeure Event, and the Parties intend this Clause to operate in a manner consistent with Section 56 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 (including the doctrine of frustration/impossibility where applicable). “Force Majeure Event” means an event beyond the reasonable control of the affected Party, which could not have been prevented by the exercise of reasonable diligence, and which prevents or materially delays performance, including: acts of God; flood; fire; earthquake; storm; lightning; epidemic or pandemic (including COVID-19 and any mutation/variant and related public health measures); war (declared or undeclared); terrorism; riot; civil commotion; embargo; and any act, order, direction, regulation, restriction, quarantine, lockdown, curfew, or shutdown imposed by any governmental authority. Force Majeure Events also include interruptions or failures of utilities, hosting infrastructure, or telecommunications services not caused by the affected Party, provided that such failures are not attributable to the affected Party’s negligence or wilful misconduct. Strikes, lockouts, and other labour disturbances are Force Majeure Events only if they are industry-wide or affect the relevant sector generally, and not if limited solely to the affected Party’s own personnel.

The affected Party will provide written notice to the other Party within {{FM_NOTICE_DAYS}} days after it becomes aware of the Force Majeure Event, describing (a) the nature of the event, (b) the obligations affected, (c) the expected impact and estimated duration (to the extent reasonably practicable), and (d) the mitigation steps being taken. The affected Party will use reasonable efforts to mitigate the impact of the Force Majeure Event, to continue performing unaffected obligations, and to resume full performance as soon as reasonably possible. The affected Party will provide written status updates at least every {{FM_UPDATE_FREQUENCY}} days during the Force Majeure Event. If partial performance is reasonably possible (including delivery of some milestones or provision of reduced/alternative services), the Parties will cooperate in good faith to permit such partial performance and to adjust the applicable SOW accordingly.

During the continuance of the Force Majeure Event, the affected obligations will be suspended (not terminated) for the period and to the extent performance is prevented or materially delayed, and the timelines will be extended day-for-day for the duration of the delay or for such longer period as is reasonably necessary to restart performance. Fees for Services that are not performed due to the Force Majeure Event will be suspended for the corresponding period, provided that {{PARTY_B_NAME}} will pay for any Services actually performed and accepted/used by {{PARTY_B_NAME}} during the Force Majeure Event (including partial Services) in accordance with the Agreement/SOW. Each Party will bear its own internal costs arising from the Force Majeure Event, unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing.

If the Force Majeure Event continues for {{FM_TERMINATION_THRESHOLD_1}} days, the Parties will meet (in person or remotely) within {{FM_MEETING_DAYS}} days to discuss in good faith commercially reasonable alternatives (including revised milestones, temporary workarounds, or a change order). If the Force Majeure Event continues for {{FM_TERMINATION_THRESHOLD_2}} days, the drafting Party may terminate the affected SOW (or, if the event materially affects the Agreement as a whole, this Agreement) by giving {{FM_TERMINATION_NOTICE}} days’ prior written notice. Upon such termination, neither Party will be liable to the other for termination arising from the Force Majeure Event, except for (a) amounts due for Services performed prior to the effective date of termination and (b) any obligations that expressly survive termination.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a provision allowing the drafting Party to terminate the affected SOW if the Force Majeure Event continues for a specified period, and clarified that the drafting Party will bear its own internal costs arising from the Force Majeure Event.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The risk has increased as the drafting Party now has more control over the termination of the affected SOW, which may lead to more disputes and potential litigation.
llm_response.legal_notes: This customization is consistent with Indian contract law, specifically Section 56 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. However, it is essential to note that the drafting Party's ability to terminate the affected SOW may be subject to challenge in court, and the Parties should carefully consider the potential risks and consequences of this provision.

llm_response.customized_text:
For purposes of this Agreement, “Pre-Existing IP” means all intellectual property and related rights (including rights in inventions, patents, copyrights, designs, trade secrets, know-how, and mask works) that a Party owns or controls prior to the [EFFECTIVE DATE] or develops independently of this Agreement without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. “Work Product” means all Deliverables and all other works, materials, developments, inventions (whether or not patentable under the Patents Act, 1970), discoveries, designs, documentation, specifications, reports, source code/object code, data compilations, and other outputs created, authored, conceived, reduced to practice, or developed by or on behalf of the Vendor in the course of performing the Services for the Client under an SOW.

Each Party retains all right, title, and interest in and to its Pre-Existing IP, and nothing in this Agreement transfers ownership of a Party’s Pre-Existing IP to the other Party. Subject to the Client’s full payment of all applicable fees, milestone charges, and approved expenses for the relevant SOW, the Work Product will vest in and be owned exclusively by the Client; until such payment is made in full, no ownership transfer will occur and the Vendor retains all right, title, and interest in the Work Product (without prejudice to any limited interim usage rights expressly granted in an SOW, if any).

To the maximum extent permitted under the Indian Copyright Act, 1957, the Parties intend that all copyright in the Work Product vests in the Client as commissioning party and that the Work Product is treated as a “work made for hire” (or equivalent vesting arrangement) for the Client; to the extent any right, title, or interest does not vest automatically, the Vendor hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to the Client, with effect upon full payment, all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product, including all worldwide intellectual property rights.

The Vendor will ensure that all its employees, consultants, and permitted subcontractors who contribute to the Work Product execute written assignments and waivers/consents consistent with this clause and the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

To the extent permitted by applicable law, the Vendor will procure waivers of all moral rights and similar rights in the Work Product; where any such rights cannot be waived under applicable law, the Vendor will procure irrevocable consents allowing the Client and its successors to use, modify, adapt, translate, publish, and otherwise exploit the Work Product without further consent and without attribution obligations, to the extent permitted.

The Vendor retains the right to use and exploit (a) general skills, know-how, experience, ideas, concepts, and techniques developed during performance (excluding Client Confidential Information), (b) anonymized and aggregated data derived from performance that does not identify the Client or any individual, and (c) improvements, enhancements, and derivatives of the Vendor’s Pre-Existing IP, tools, methodologies, libraries, and frameworks, provided such retained rights do not permit the Vendor to disclose or use the Client’s Confidential Information.

If any Work Product includes, is based on, or requires the Vendor’s Pre-Existing IP or any third-party materials to enable the Client to use the Work Product as intended, then the Vendor grants (and/or will procure for the Client) a [{{LICENSE_TYPE}}] license to such incorporated Pre-Existing IP and/or third-party materials, solely to the extent necessary for the Client to use, operate, maintain, and receive the benefit of the Work Product for its intended purpose, and subject to any usage restrictions stated in the SOW.

The Client retains all right, title, and interest in and to all materials, data, content, and other items provided by the Client (“Client Materials”); the Client grants the Vendor a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license during the Term to use the Client Materials solely as necessary to perform the Services.

Any third-party materials to be incorporated will be identified in the applicable SOW (including applicable license terms), and the Vendor represents that it has the right to provide and incorporate such third-party materials as described, subject to the third-party license terms.

The Vendor represents and warrants that, excluding Client Materials and third-party materials identified in the SOW, the Work Product as delivered will not knowingly infringe any third-party intellectual property rights (with any remedies, indemnities, exclusions, and limitations to be as set out elsewhere in this Agreement).

Each Party will execute and deliver, and will cause its personnel to execute and deliver, such further instruments and take such further actions as reasonably requested to evidence, perfect, or enforce the rights and transfers contemplated by this clause, including executing assignments and supporting filings/registrations under the Indian Copyright Act, 1957 and the Patents Act, 1970.

Except as expressly set out in this clause, no license or other rights are granted by either Party, whether by implication, estoppel, or otherwise.

Subject to the Exceptions and Special Caps below, {{PARTY_A_NAME}}'s total aggregate liability arising out of or relating to this Agreement (including any SOW) will not exceed the lesser of (a) {{LIABILITY_CAP_AMOUNT}} and (b) the total fees actually paid under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the first event giving rise to the claim. The cap in the preceding sentence is an aggregate cap that applies across all claims, actions, and causes of action (whether in contract, tort (including negligence), breach of statutory duty, restitution, or otherwise). Neither {{PARTY_A_NAME}} nor {{PARTY_B_NAME}} will be liable to the other for any indirect, consequential, special, punitive, exemplary, or incidental damages. Without limiting the foregoing, neither {{PARTY_A_NAME}} nor {{PARTY_B_NAME}} will be liable for loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of business opportunity, loss of goodwill, business interruption, or loss, corruption, or restoration of data, arising out of or relating to this Agreement, even if such loss was foreseeable or the Party was advised of the possibility of such damages.

The Parties agree that any service credits expressly stated in an SOW (if applicable) are intended as a genuine pre-estimate and a reasonable contractual adjustment for service level failures, consistent with Section 74 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, and are the exclusive remedy for breach of SLAs to the extent stated in the SOW. The following “Special Caps” will apply instead of the general cap above, as applicable: (a) for a data security incident or personal data breach caused by a Party, that Party’s aggregate liability will not exceed {{DATA_BREACH_CAP}}; (b) for breach of confidentiality obligations, the breaching Party’s aggregate liability will not exceed {{CONFIDENTIALITY_CAP}}; and (c) for infringement or misappropriation of third-party intellectual property by a Party, that Party’s aggregate liability will not exceed {{IP_INFRINGEMENT_CAP}}. The “Exceptions” (not subject to the general cap or the Special Caps) are liability to the extent finally determined to arise from a Party’s fraud, wilful misconduct, or gross negligence. In addition, {{PARTY_B_NAME}}'s payment obligations and each Party’s indemnity obligations in favour of the other Party under this Agreement (including third-party claims) will not be subject to the caps in this Clause, unless expressly stated otherwise in the indemnity provision. Each Party will take reasonable steps to mitigate its losses. This Clause will survive termination or expiry of the Agreement.

clause_risks.explanation: This risk level indicates that there's a moderate chance of disputes arising from unclear or incomplete definitions. It's essential to ensure that all definitions are clearly defined and that all parties understand their obligations.
clause_risks.mitigation: Clearly define all definitions and ensure that all parties understand their obligations.

llm_response.customized_text: Definitions. ‘Confidential Information’ means all non-public information disclosed by {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}} (each, a ‘Disclosing Party’) to the other (each, a ‘Receiving Party’) in connection with this Agreement, including: (a) technical information (software, source code, architecture, documentation, specifications, designs, algorithms, know-how); (b) commercial information (pricing, proposals, customer lists, contracts, supplier details, marketing and sales plans); (c) security measures and vulnerabilities; and (d) any analyses, compilations, notes, or materials derived from the foregoing. Confidentiality; Use Restriction. During the Term and for {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}} thereafter, the Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Permitted Purpose and strictly on a need-to-know basis; (b) keep Confidential Information confidential and protect it using at least reasonable care, including the handling requirements in Clause 4; and (c) not disclose any Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, except as expressly permitted in this Clause. Disclosures to Representatives. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives solely for the Permitted Purpose, provided that: (a) such Representatives are subject to written confidentiality obligations no less protective than this Clause; (b) access is limited to those who need to know; and (c) the Receiving Party remains fully liable for any breach by its Representatives. Handling Requirements. The Receiving Party will implement and maintain appropriate safeguards, including: (a) access controls and role-based permissions for electronic access; (b) storage of Confidential Information using secure systems and encryption in transit (and, where reasonable, at rest); (c) no downloading, printing, or copying except to the extent necessary for the Permitted Purpose and in accordance with internal access controls; and (d) prompt marking/labeling of printed materials as confidential and secure disposal (shredding or equivalent) when no longer needed. Prior Notice for Legally Mandated Disclosure. If the Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court/tribunal to disclose Confidential Information, it may do so only to the extent required, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) it gives the Disclosing Party at least [10–15] days’ prior written notice (or, if not practicable, as much notice as reasonably possible) and reasonably cooperates (at the Disclosing Party’s cost) with the Disclosing Party’s efforts to obtain confidential treatment or a protective order. Exclusions. Confidential Information excludes information that the Receiving Party demonstrates by written records falls within any of the exclusions listed in sub-clauses (a)–(d) under the Standard Variant above (public domain, prior possession, third-party lawful receipt, independent development). Survival. This Clause will survive expiry or termination of this Agreement for the period specified as {{CONFIDENTIALITY_PERIOD}}, and will continue to apply to any Confidential Information disclosed before expiry or termination. No Restraint of Trade. The parties agree that this Clause is intended solely to protect Confidential Information and does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, consistent with Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a more stringent notice period for legally mandated disclosure to protect the drafting party’s interests.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The increased notice period for legally mandated disclosure may lead to higher risks for the drafting party if the receiving party fails to comply, potentially resulting in reputational damage or financial losses.
llm_response.legal_notes: This customization maintains enforceability under the Indian Contract Act, 1872, by ensuring that the clause does not restrict either party from conducting its trade or business generally, except to prevent misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information.

llm_response.customized_text: “Agreement” means this service contract executed between {{PARTY_A_NAME}} and {{PARTY_B_NAME}}, together with all schedules, annexures, statements of work (“SOWs”), change requests (if any), and amendments executed in writing. The Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, and the courts of [CITY], India shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. “Business Day” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or public holiday on which banks are generally open for business in [CITY], India. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed (directly or indirectly) by or on behalf of a Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement, in any form (including oral, written, electronic, visual), that is marked or identified as confidential or that reasonably should be understood as confidential. Confidential Information includes, by way of example: pricing and commercial terms, business plans, customer and supplier lists, product roadmaps, technical specifications, source code, system architecture, security measures, processes, and data compilations. “Deliverables” means the tangible or intangible outputs to be delivered under the Services, as specified in the applicable SOW or schedule, including reports, documents, specifications, designs, software, configurations, datasets, or other work results. “Effective Date” means [EFFECTIVE DATE]. “Fees” means the charges payable for the Services (including any one-time, milestone-based, time-and-material, recurring, or reimbursable amounts), as set out in this Agreement and/or the applicable SOW, excluding taxes unless expressly stated otherwise. “Force Majeure” means an event or circumstance beyond the reasonable control of a Party that prevents or materially delays performance of its obligations, including acts of God, flood, fire, earthquake, epidemic/pandemic, war, terrorism, riots, civil commotion, strikes or industrial disputes (excluding strikes of the affected Party’s own personnel unless industry-wide), governmental orders, or interruption of utilities or telecommunications not caused by the affected Party. “Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property and similar rights, whether registered or unregistered, including copyrights, moral rights, trademarks, service marks, trade names, logos, patents, industrial designs, know-how, confidential information to the extent protectable, and all applications, renewals, and extensions of such rights. “Party” means either {{PARTY_A_NAME}} or {{PARTY_B_NAME}}, as applicable. “Parties” means both collectively. “Representative” means a Party’s directors, officers, employees, consultants, professional advisers, subcontractors, and agents who have a need to know the relevant information for purposes of this Agreement. “Services” means the services to be performed by [SERVICE PROVIDER] for [CUSTOMER] under this Agreement, as described in the applicable SOW(s), including any associated implementation, support, maintenance, or advisory activities expressly agreed in writing. “Term” means the period commencing on the Effective Date and continuing for [TERM LENGTH], unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement. “Territory” means [INDIA / STATE(S) / GLOBAL / AS SPECIFIED IN SOW], being the geographic scope (if any) within which the Services and/or Deliverables are to be performed or used, as stated in this Agreement or the applicable SOW.

llm_response.customized_text:
Any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including its existence, validity, interpretation, performance, breach, or termination (a “Dispute”), will be resolved in accordance with this clause and the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, as amended (the “Act”). Either Party may initiate the process by giving the other Party a written notice describing the Dispute in reasonable detail.

First, the Parties will attempt to resolve the Dispute through escalation and good-faith negotiations. The Dispute will be escalated to [{{ESCALATION_CONTACTS}}] (or their equivalents) and the Parties will meet (in person or by video conference) within [MEETING_DAYS] days of the notice. If unresolved, the Dispute will be escalated to senior executives of each Party, who will attempt to resolve it within [{{ESCALATION_PERIOD}}] days at each escalation level (or such other period agreed in writing).

If the Dispute remains unresolved after escalation, either Party may require the Parties to attempt mediation for a period of [{{MEDIATION_PERIOD}}] days. Mediation will be conducted under [{{MEDIATION_RULES}}] or, if not specified, with a mutually agreed mediator appointed on terms agreed by the Parties. Participation in mediation does not prevent either Party from seeking interim relief as permitted under the Act, and any settlement reached will be recorded in writing and signed by both Parties.

If the Dispute is not resolved by negotiation/mediation (or if mediation is declined or expires), the Dispute will be finally resolved by arbitration. The arbitration will be administered under the rules of [{{ARBITRATION_INSTITUTION}}] (or, if the institution declines or is unavailable, as an ad hoc arbitration under the Act), and the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with such rules to the extent they are consistent with the Act. The seat (legal place) of arbitration will be [{{ARBITRATION_SEAT}}], India, and the tribunal may hold hearings by video conference or at any convenient location, without changing the seat. The tribunal will consist of [{{NUMBER_ARBITRATORS}}] arbitrator(s); if three arbitrators, each Party will appoint one and the presiding arbitrator will be appointed by agreement of the two party-appointed arbitrators or, failing agreement, by the institution in accordance with its rules. The arbitration will be conducted in English. The tribunal may grant interim measures and the Parties may seek interim or conservatory relief from the tribunal and/or competent courts as permitted under the Act. The tribunal will give a reasoned award, and the award will be final and binding and enforceable in accordance with the Act and applicable law. The tribunal will allocate the costs of arbitration, including the tribunal’s fees and expenses and (to the extent permitted) reasonable legal fees, in accordance with the applicable rules and/or as the tribunal determines. The Parties will keep the arbitration proceedings, filings, evidence, and award confidential except to the extent disclosure is required by law, to enforce the award, or to professional advisers/insurers bound by confidentiality.

If the drafting Party is not satisfied with the resolution process, it may terminate this Agreement with immediate effect by giving written notice to the other Party.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a termination clause for the drafting Party's benefit, allowing them to terminate the Agreement if they are not satisfied with the resolution process.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The added termination clause increases the risk of termination, which may impact the enforceability of the Agreement. However, this change is consistent with Indian contract law, which allows for termination in certain circumstances.
llm_response.legal_notes: This customization is consistent with Indian contract law, specifically the Indian Contract Act 1872. The added termination clause is a common provision in contracts to protect the drafting Party's interests. However, it is essential to note that termination may impact the enforceability of the Agreement, and the Parties should carefully consider this provision before agreeing to it.

llm_response.customized_text: This Agreement, together with all SOWs, schedules, exhibits, and appendices expressly incorporated by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous proposals, bids, discussions, negotiations, understandings, and agreements, whether oral or written. Each Party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it has not relied on any representation, warranty, statement, or promise not expressly set out in this Agreement. In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and an SOW, the SOW will prevail only for the specific Services/Deliverables and commercial terms expressly covered by that SOW, and this Agreement will prevail for all general terms. The drafting Party reserves the right to modify or update this Agreement at any time, and such modifications will be effective upon written notice to the other Party. No amendment, modification, or supplement to this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties; amendments exchanged by email will be effective only if sent by the Parties’ authorized signatories and expressly state an intention to amend this Agreement. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law (including the Indian Contract Act, 1872), that provision will be severed and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid or unenforceable provision with a valid provision that, to the maximum extent permitted by law, achieves the original commercial intent and allocates risk in a substantially similar manner. The drafting Party will not be liable for any losses or damages arising from any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy under this Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted electronically (including PDF) will be treated as original signatures to the extent permitted by applicable law.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a provision allowing the drafting Party to modify or update the Agreement at any time, and also included a liability limitation for the drafting Party in case of delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy.
llm_response.risk_explanation: The added provision allowing the drafting Party to modify or update the Agreement at any time may increase the risk of disputes or misunderstandings between the Parties. Additionally, the liability limitation for the drafting Party may shift more risk to the other Party in case of delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy.
llm_response.legal_notes: The added provision allowing the drafting Party to modify or update the Agreement at any time may be subject to the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which requires that any modification or variation to a contract must be in writing and signed by both Parties. The liability limitation for the drafting Party may also be subject to the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which requires that any limitation of liability must be reasonable and not contrary to public policy.

This Agreement, together with all SOWs, schedules, exhibits, and appendices expressly incorporated by reference, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous proposals, bids, discussions, negotiations, understandings, and agreements, whether oral or written. Each Party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it has not relied on any representation, warranty, statement, or promise not expressly set out in this Agreement. In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and an SOW, the SOW will prevail only for the specific Services/Deliverables and commercial terms expressly covered by that SOW, and this Agreement will prevail for all general terms.

No amendment, modification, or supplement to this Agreement will be effective unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties; amendments exchanged by email will be effective only if sent by the Parties’ authorized signatories and expressly state an intention to amend this Agreement. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law (including the Indian Contract Act, 1872), that provision will be severed and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The Parties will negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid or unenforceable provision with a valid provision that, to the maximum extent permitted by law, achieves the original commercial intent and allocates risk in a substantially similar manner.

No waiver of any provision of this Agreement will be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party granting the waiver. A waiver of any breach will not be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach, and any delay or failure to exercise a right or remedy will not operate as a waiver of that right or remedy. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted electronically (including PDF) will be treated as original signatures to the extent permitted by applicable law.

llm_response.customized_text: This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to it will be governed by the substantive laws of India. The application of conflict of laws principles is excluded. Subject to any mandatory statutory provisions, the Parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located at [{{JURISDICTION_CITY}}], [{{STATE}}]. Each Party waives any objection to the laying of venue in such courts and any claim that such proceedings have been brought in an inconvenient forum, to the extent permitted by law. Service of process, notices, and other court communications may be effected by email and/or courier to the addresses set out in this Agreement, in addition to any other method permitted by applicable law. Service by courier will be deemed received on delivery as per courier tracking, and service by email will be deemed received on the date of transmission (provided no bounce-back or delivery failure message is received). In addition, the drafting Party reserves the right to seek interim or injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction where such relief is necessary to protect its rights pending final determination.

llm_response.customized_text:
{{PARTY_B_NAME}} (the ‘Client’) will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless {{PARTY_A_NAME}} (the ‘Vendor’), its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claims, demands, actions, suits, or proceedings arising out of or relating to: (a) any data, content, instructions, materials, or software provided by or on behalf of the Client (‘Client Materials’), including allegations that the Client Materials infringe, misappropriate, or violate third-party rights; (b) the Client’s use of the Services or Deliverables outside the scope, limitations, or permitted purpose stated in this Agreement or an SOW; and (c) any combination of the Services or Deliverables with the Client’s systems, products, data, or processes, to the extent the claim would not have arisen but for such combination. Vendor will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client and its directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claim alleging that the Services or Deliverables, when used by the Client in accordance with this Agreement and the applicable SOW, infringe any third-party intellectual property right.
The Party seeking indemnity (the ‘Indemnified Party’) will provide written notice of any indemnifiable claim within {{INDEMNITY_NOTICE_DAYS}} days of becoming aware of it, provided that any failure to give timely notice will relieve the Indemnifying Party of its obligations only to the extent it is materially prejudiced by such failure. The Indemnifying Party will have the right to control the defence and settlement of the claim using counsel reasonably acceptable to the Indemnified Party. The Indemnifying Party will not settle any claim in a manner that (i) imposes any admission of liability, fault, or wrongdoing on the Indemnified Party, or (ii) requires the Indemnified Party to take or refrain from taking any action (other than ceasing use of allegedly infringing items), without the Indemnified Party’s prior written consent. The Indemnified Party will provide reasonable cooperation and assistance as requested, and the Indemnifying Party will reimburse reasonable out-of-pocket costs incurred for such cooperation. The Indemnified Party may participate in the defence with counsel of its own choosing at its own expense. The Indemnified Party will not admit liability, compromise, or settle any indemnified claim without the Indemnifying Party’s prior written consent.
This Clause will not apply to the extent a claim arises from the Indemnified Party’s breach of this Agreement, negligence, or wilful misconduct. The indemnity obligations in this Clause are subject to the Limitation of Liability clause. This Clause will survive termination or expiry of the Agreement.
llm_response.changes_summary: Added a sentence to clarify that the Vendor will also indemnify the Client against third-party claims alleging infringement of intellectual property rights. Vendor will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client and its directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against any third-party claim alleging that the Services or Deliverables, when used by the Client in accordance with this Agreement and the applicable SOW, infringe any third-party intellectual property right.

The Party seeking indemnity (the “Indemnified Party”) will provide written notice of any indemnifiable claim within {{INDEMNITY_NOTICE_DAYS}} days of becoming aware of it, provided that any failure to give timely notice will relieve the Indemnifying Party of its obligations only to the extent it is materially prejudiced by such failure. The Indemnifying Party will have the right to control the defence and settlement of the claim using counsel reasonably acceptable to the Indemnified Party. The Indemnifying Party will not settle any claim in a manner that (i) imposes any admission of liability, fault, or wrongdoing on the Indemnified Party, or (ii) requires the Indemnified Party to take or refrain from taking any action (other than ceasing use of allegedly infringing items), without the Indemnified Party’s prior written consent. The Indemnified Party will provide reasonable cooperation and assistance as requested, and the Indemnifying Party will reimburse reasonable out-of-pocket costs incurred for such cooperation. The Indemnified Party may participate in the defence with counsel of its own choosing at its own expense. The Indemnified Party will not admit liability, compromise, or settle any indemnified claim without the Indemnifying Party’s prior written consent.

The indemnities in this Clause will not apply to the extent a claim arises from the Indemnified Party’s breach of this Agreement, negligence, or wilful misconduct. Except to the extent expressly stated otherwise in this Agreement, the indemnity obligations in this Clause are subject to the Limitation of Liability clause. This Clause will survive termination or expiry of the Agreement.
